name: Nan (Indian Bread)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon poppy seeds or sesame seeds
recipeInstructions:
1. Warm the yogurt and stir in the milk until thoroughly mixed. Remove from heat and cool.
2. In a mixing bowl, add the yogurt mixture to an electric mixer with dough hook attachment, or mix by hand. Add baking soda, sugar, 4 tablespoons butter, eggs, and yeast. Mix until the yeast dissolves.
3. Stir in flour and salt. Knead the dough for 15-20 minutes until smooth and elastic.
4. Place the dough on a plastic container and cover with a large stainless-steel bowl. Let the dough rise to twice its size, about 2 hours.
5. Dust hands with flour, knead the dough briefly, and divide into 8 balls.
6. Roll each ball into a 10-inch pancake. Gently shape each pancake into an oval form.
7. Cover with plastic wrap for 20 minutes. Do not stack the pancakes as they will stick together.
8. Preheat oven to 450°F (232°C) with clay tiles or a pizza stone, or use an upside-down baking sheet.
9. Mix remaining 4 tablespoons butter with poppy seeds on a small plywood board dusted with flour.
10. Place a loaf on the board and brush with some of the butter and seeds mixture.
11. Slide into the oven and bake until bubbles on the dough turn slightly brown, about 4 minutes.
12. Remove from oven using the plywood board. Brush the bottom of the loaf with water.
13. Stack loaves and cover with plastic wrap until serving time.
Note: Flash freezing is an option if you want to preserve the bread for later use.
